title: notholaena aurea
intro: Notholaena aurea, also known as the golden lipfern or golden polypody, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Notholaena aurea include reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and antioxidant activity, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Notholaena aurea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in conjunction with other medicinal herbs in traditional folk medicine.
